title: Convert io::Error with From
scenario: A From implementation centralizes how low-level I/O errors become configuration errors and enables ? at call sites.
instructions: Implement From<std::io::Error> for ConfigLoadError by returning ConfigLoadError::FileRead(error).

hints.body: The starter already stores io::Error in FileRead. Add a From<io::Error> implementation for ConfigLoadError.
The from method should take the io::Error by value and return ConfigLoadError::FileRead(error).
One complete solution for Convert io::Error with From is shown below.

completionExplanation: ConfigLoadError can now be constructed from io::Error, which prepares loader code to use ? for file-reading failures.
